title: SOP – Snap-freezing fly heads

materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"21nmg","text":"- Eppendorf tubes (1.5 or 2mL)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"rpiim","text":"- Biomasher II Tube/Pest, Sterile – KIMBLE (Cat #9749625002)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cojgs","text":"- Ice bucket","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9o00g","text":"- Glass vial","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"puxss","text":"- Small funnel","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"y0lbo","text":"- Liquid nitrogen","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"nkfex","text":"- Vortex","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"w0wcv","text":"- Bigger plastic petri dish","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"nwyhe","text":"- Smaller glass petri dish","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"mayp2","text":"- Forceps","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"sjlkb","text":"- Microscope","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eowoh","text":"- Marker","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Label each Eppendorf tube and each Biomasher tube.
Pour ice into a plastic petri dish, and rest the glass petri dish on the ice, rim up. *This will ensure that all the fly heads stay on a glass surface during step 8.*
Put a glass vial with a small funnel in ice. Make sure it’s very cold before the next step.

Tap down the vial with flies so that all the flies are at the bottom of the vial. Open the vial and invert it onto the funnel+glass vial. Tap the whole thing down to ensure all the flies are transferred from their original vial into a cold glass vial. Flies should quickly fall asleep.
Funnel the anaesthetized flies into their respective Eppendorf tubes.

Freeze the Eppendorf into liquid nitrogen. TIP: Swivel and shake the Eppendorf to ensure even freezing. Keep the tube in liquid nitrogen until it stops bubbling to ensure that the flies are fully frozen.

Switch the vortex into the ON mode (not the TOUCH mode) at its highest speed. Vortex the frozen Eppendorf. This will separate the fly heads from their bodies. TIP: Aim to get rough, abrupt, and violent contact between the Eppendorf and the vortex and minimize the delay between having the tube in the liquid nitrogen and on the vortex to achieve effective decapitation.

Dump the contents of the Eppendorf onto the iced glass petri dish. Check the Eppendorf under the microscope to ensure that all the heads are out of the tube. TIP: The colder the tube, the easier it will empty out, so do not vortex for too long.

Using a microscope and forceps, collect fly heads into the Biomasher tube. Make sure they are below the top line before closing the tube to avoid mashing the heads. TIP: Sometimes, especially upon warming up, heads stick to the forceps. The easiest way to get heads into the tube in that case is to “wipe” the heads off the rim of the tube. After you have the desired amount of heads on the rims, “poke” the heads into the tube.
Clean the glass petri dish with ethanol.
creator: Janna Jernigan Posey
